questions.q: What is the process by which an electric current is used to drive a chemical reaction in the direction opposite to that in which it would occur spontaneously?
opts: (A) Electrolysis
(B) Electrosynthesis
(C) Electrodeposition
(D) Electrochemical reaction
questions.explanation: Electrolysis is the process by which an electric current is used to drive a chemical reaction in the direction opposite to that in which it would occur spontaneously. The other options are related to electrochemistry but are not the correct answer to this question.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of an electrochemical cell?
opts: (A) Battery
(B) Electrolytic cell
(C) Galvanic cell
(D) All of the above
questions.explanation: All of the above options are examples of electrochemical cells. A battery is a device that generates an electric current through an electrochemical reaction, an electrolytic cell is a device that uses an electric current to drive a chemical reaction, and a galvanic cell is a type of electrochemical cell that generates an electric current through a spontaneous redox reaction.

questions.q: What is the standard reduction potential of a half-cell?
opts: (A) The potential of the half-cell when the concentration of the oxidized and reduced species are equal
(B) The potential of the half-cell when the concentration of the oxidized species is greater than the concentration of the reduced species
(C) The potential of the half-cell when the concentration of the reduced species is greater than the concentration of the oxidized species
(D) The potential of the half-cell when the concentration of the oxidized and reduced species are zero
questions.explanation: The standard reduction potential of a half-cell is the potential of the half-cell when the concentration of the oxidized and reduced species are equal, and it is measured under standard conditions.

questions.q: What is the relationship between the standard reduction potential and the spontaneity of a redox reaction?
opts: (A) A positive standard reduction potential indicates a non-spontaneous reaction
(B) A negative standard reduction potential indicates a non-spontaneous reaction
(C) A positive standard reduction potential indicates a spontaneous reaction
(D) A negative standard reduction potential indicates a spontaneous reaction
questions.explanation: A positive standard reduction potential indicates a spontaneous reaction, while a negative standard reduction potential indicates a non-spontaneous reaction.

questions.q: What is the function of a salt bridge in an electrochemical cell?
opts: (A) To conduct electrons between the two half-cells
(B) To conduct ions between the two half-cells
(C) To separate the two half-cells
(D) To generate an electric current
questions.explanation: The function of a salt bridge in an electrochemical cell is to conduct ions between the two half-cells, allowing the cell to function as a single unit.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a galvanic cell?
opts: (A) A battery
(B) An electrolytic cell
(C) A cell that generates an electric current through a spontaneous redox reaction
(D) A cell that uses an electric current to drive a chemical reaction
questions.explanation: A galvanic cell is a type of electrochemical cell that generates an electric current through a spontaneous redox reaction.

opts: (A) An equation that relates the standard reduction potential of a half-cell to the concentration of the oxidized and reduced species
(B) An equation that relates the standard reduction potential of a half-cell to the temperature of the half-cell
(C) An equation that relates the standard reduction potential of a half-cell to the pressure of the half-cell
(D) An equation that relates the standard reduction potential of a half-cell to the volume of the half-cell
questions.explanation: The Nernst equation is an equation that relates the standard reduction potential of a half-cell to the concentration of the oxidized and reduced species.

questions.q: What is the effect of increasing the concentration of the oxidized species on the potential of a half-cell?
opts: (A) The potential of the half-cell increases
(B) The potential of the half-cell decreases
(C) The potential of the half-cell remains the same
(D) The potential of the half-cell becomes zero
questions.explanation: Increasing the concentration of the oxidized species increases the potential of the half-cell.

questions.q: What is the effect of decreasing the concentration of the reduced species on the potential of a half-cell?
opts: (A) The potential of the half-cell increases
(B) The potential of the half-cell decreases
(C) The potential of the half-cell remains the same
(D) The potential of the half-cell becomes zero
questions.explanation: Decreasing the concentration of the reduced species increases the potential of the half-cell.

questions.q: What is the relationship between the standard reduction potential and the equilibrium constant of a redox reaction?
opts: (A) A positive standard reduction potential indicates a large equilibrium constant
(B) A negative standard reduction potential indicates a large equilibrium constant
(C) A positive standard reduction potential indicates a small equilibrium constant
(D) A negative standard reduction potential indicates a small equilibrium constant
questions.explanation: A positive standard reduction potential indicates a large equilibrium constant, while a negative standard reduction potential indicates a small equilibrium constant.

questions.q: What is the function of a reference electrode in an electrochemical cell?
opts: (A) To conduct electrons between the two half-cells
(B) To conduct ions between the two half-cells
(C) To provide a reference potential for the cell
(D) To generate an electric current
questions.explanation: The function of a reference electrode in an electrochemical cell is to provide a reference potential for the cell, allowing the potential of the other electrode to be measured.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of an electrolytic cell?
opts: (A) A battery
(B) A galvanic cell
(C) A cell that uses an electric current to drive a chemical reaction
(D) A cell that generates an electric current through a spontaneous redox reaction
questions.explanation: An electrolytic cell is a type of electrochemical cell that uses an electric current to drive a chemical reaction.

questions.q: What is the Faraday's law of electrolysis?
opts: (A) The amount of substance deposited is directly proportional to the quantity of electricity passed
(B) The amount of substance deposited is inversely proportional to the quantity of electricity passed
(C) The amount of substance deposited is directly proportional to the time of electrolysis
(D) The amount of substance deposited is inversely proportional to the time of electrolysis
questions.explanation: The Faraday's law of electrolysis states that the amount of substance deposited is directly proportional to the quantity of electricity passed.

questions.q: What is the relationship between the quantity of electricity passed and the amount of substance deposited during electrolysis?
opts: (A) The amount of substance deposited is directly proportional to the quantity of electricity passed
(B) The amount of substance deposited is inversely proportional to the quantity of electricity passed
(C) The amount of substance deposited is directly proportional to the square root of the quantity of electricity passed
(D) The amount of substance deposited is inversely proportional to the square root of the quantity of electricity passed
questions.explanation: The amount of substance deposited is directly proportional to the quantity of electricity passed, as stated by the Faraday's law of electrolysis.

questions.q: What is the function of a fuse in an electrochemical cell?
opts: (A) To conduct electrons between the two half-cells
(B) To conduct ions between the two half-cells
(C) To prevent excessive current from flowing through the cell
(D) To generate an electric current
questions.explanation: The function of a fuse in an electrochemical cell is to prevent excessive current from flowing through the cell, protecting the cell from damage.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of a galvanic cell?
opts: (A) It uses an electric current to drive a chemical reaction
(B) It generates an electric current through a spontaneous redox reaction
(C) It has a negative standard reduction potential
(D) It has a zero standard reduction potential
questions.explanation: A galvanic cell generates an electric current through a spontaneous redox reaction, which is a characteristic of this type of cell.

questions.q: What is the relationship between the standard reduction potential and the Gibbs free energy change of a redox reaction?
opts: (A) A positive standard reduction potential indicates a positive Gibbs free energy change
(B) A negative standard reduction potential indicates a positive Gibbs free energy change
(C) A positive standard reduction potential indicates a negative Gibbs free energy change
(D) A negative standard reduction potential indicates a negative Gibbs free energy change
questions.explanation: A positive standard reduction potential indicates a negative Gibbs free energy change, while a negative standard reduction potential indicates a positive Gibbs free energy change.

questions.q: What is the effect of increasing the temperature on the standard reduction potential of a half-cell?
opts: (A) The standard reduction potential increases
(B) The standard reduction potential decreases
(C) The standard reduction potential remains the same
(D) The standard reduction potential becomes zero
questions.explanation: Increasing the temperature decreases the standard reduction potential of a half-cell.

questions.q: What is the relationship between the standard reduction potential and the entropy change of a redox reaction?
opts: (A) A positive standard reduction potential indicates a positive entropy change
(B) A negative standard reduction potential indicates a positive entropy change
(C) A positive standard reduction potential indicates a negative entropy change
(D) A negative standard reduction potential indicates a negative entropy change
questions.explanation: A positive standard reduction potential indicates a positive entropy change, while a negative standard reduction potential indicates a negative entropy change.
